description: Vintage five-joint mohair teddy — cleaning and refresh, restuffing, and sewing and fabric repair.
itemDetails:
A vintage five-joint mohair teddy belongs to the classic articulated bear tradition, usually inspired by early 20th-century German and British bear-making patterns. Five-joint construction (head, arms, legs) is a key hallmark for older collector-style teddy design.

Materials are commonly mohair pile on backing, with wood-wool or fiber stuffing in older examples and disk/cotter-pin joints for articulation. Market value can range widely, with maker attribution, age evidence, and originality of eyes/pads/stitching driving collector demand.
